work_unit: SZ-SP-M-004: Progress listener race condition — interleaved events from concurrent analyses
situation: SidePanelApp.tsx onMessage listener receives all pipelineProgress broadcasts globally with no correlation filtering. If two analyses trigger rapidly, both listeners receive each other's progress events, causing missed/interleaved updates and stale debug info.
action: Add unique requestId per analysis run. Sidepanel generates req-{timestamp}-{random} and sends with runPipeline message. Background echoes requestId in every pipelineProgress broadcast. Sidepanel filters progress events by matching requestId. Used case-104 direct wt commit escape hatch (cp-guard triggered because opt-sidepanel-1782907225 had prior unmerged id-delete-fix divergence).
outcome_evidence: commit 1bcf11f on auto/opt-sidepanel-1782907225, pushed to origin. 2 files changed, +8/-1 lines. requestId generated in handleAnalyze, echoed in background onProgress, filtered in progressListener.
next_suggestion: Next pending finding: SZ-PP-M-001 (FileReader has no onerror handler — silent failure on read errors). Then SZ-PP-M-002 through SZ-PP-M-006 (popup medium fixes batch).

audit_findings.finding: onMessage is a global broadcast channel with no correlation ID filtering. Concurrent analyses interleave progress events.
audit_findings.remediation: Add unique requestId per analysis; filter pipelineProgress by matching requestId.
